content:
It’s not the skills gap.

12 min read
Just now

--

Read the article for free here.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

If you have applied to AI roles recently and heard nothing back, here is what is happening on the other side.
Karat: the platform that runs technical interviews for a lot of tech companies — reported in April 2026 that take-home coding assignments (the ones companies send you to finish at home during interviews) have lost their hiring signal faster than any other filter under AI. When you ship a standard RAG demo, a hiring manager sees the same artifact a motivated non-engineer with a Claude Pro subscription produces over a weekend.
The fix is not another RAG demo. It’s a signed artifact: one shipped agent you maintained through its failures.
That is the gap. It isn’t the skills.
The market you think isn’t hiring is actually hiring
It is easy to look at the silence in your inbox and assume the AI bubble popped. The data says the opposite. LinkedIn is now processing 11,000 applications a minute. The Indeed Hiring Lab reported in January 2026 that AI-mentioning postings are 134% above pre-pandemic levels. Larger tech companies are showing 50% to 100% more AI engineering listings than they did a year ago.
